question_text: A ball is dropped from a height of 10 metres. Ignoring air resistance, what is the total mechanical energy of the ball just before it hits the ground compared to when it was released?

options.label: A
options.text: The total mechanical energy is the same

options.label: B
options.text: The total mechanical energy increases because the ball is moving faster

options.label: C
options.text: The total mechanical energy decreases because some energy is lost

options.label: D
options.text: The total mechanical energy doubles because the ball falls 10 metres

explanation: Energy is conserved in a falling object. All the gravitational potential energy at the top converts to kinetic energy at the bottom, so the total mechanical energy remains constant when we ignore air resistance.
